meta_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve no project ideas (Advanced Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no project ideas. A Advanced-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Analytics Setup to Solve 'No Project Ideas'
content.seo_description: Stop guessing. Learn how to use analytics to identify high-value SaaS problems. A technical guide for founders using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: The 'no project idea' trap is a data deficiency, not a creative one. By implementing event-driven analytics into a low-code MVP, you capture real-time friction points from your target audience. This creates a feedback loop that validates market demand before you commit to a full-scale build.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the graveyard of ambition. You are staring at a cursor, paralyzed by the fear that your next SaaS build will be another ghost town. Most founders treat 'idea generation' as an act of artistic inspiration, but the reality is colder: it is a data engineering problem disguised as a creative one.

To move from zero to revenue, you must stop 'thinking' and start 'tracking.' By instrumenting your environment with robust analytics, you transform passive observation into actionable business intelligence. We are going to deploy an event-tracking infrastructure that turns your user's friction into your next million-dollar feature.

steps.title: Define High-Value Event Schemas
steps.description: Before writing code, define the interaction points that indicate user intent. We focus on 'pain-proxy' events like repeated search failures or abandoned workflows.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a JSON schema for tracking user interaction events in a SaaS MVP, specifically focusing on 'feature discovery' and 'workflow abandonment' metrics.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Create a tracking-schema.json file based on the Antigravity output and integrate it into the src/analytics directory.'

steps.title: Inject Analytics Middleware
steps.description: We wrap your core application logic in an analytics middleware to ensure every user action is captured without polluting your business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an analytics middleware for a Next.js/Node.js stack that intercepts API requests and logs events to a local buffer.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Implement the middleware in the current project and wrap the main API router.'

steps.title: Automate Data Visualization
steps.description: Raw logs are useless. We need an automated dashboard to surface patterns in user behavior that define your next product pivot.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a lightweight dashboard component using Recharts to visualize daily event frequency and user drop-off rates.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Add the dashboard component to /admin/analytics and ensure it pulls from the local event log.'

steps.title: Implement Anomaly Alerts
steps.description: Don't stare at charts. Let the system tell you when a potential business opportunity arises via an automated notification.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a script that monitors event logs for spikes in specific error codes or repeated user actions and triggers a Discord/Slack webhook.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Deploy the anomaly detection script as a cron job in the current environment.'

steps.title: Validate Market Demand
steps.description: Use the gathered intelligence to iterate. If users keep trying to click a non-existent feature, you have your next project idea.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a prompt for Claude to analyze the last 24 hours of logs and suggest the top 3 most requested features based on user friction data.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Execute the analysis prompt against the current logs and summarize the findings in a README.md file.'

pro_tips: Track the 'rage click'—if a user clicks a button three times in under two seconds, you've found a broken UX or a hidden demand.
Ignore vanity metrics like page views; focus entirely on 'time-to-first-value' (TTFV) metrics.
Use ephemeral databases for early-stage analytics to keep your production costs near zero.
Implement 'feature flags' alongside analytics so you can turn off failing experiments without redeploying.
Always log the 'context' of an error, not just the error code; include the user's last three steps to see the path of destruction.
resources: Analytics Best Practices for SaaS: https://segment.com/academy
content.next_steps: Deploy your telemetry-enabled MVP to a small test group on Reddit or IndieHackers, monitor the logs for 48 hours, and let the data dictate your next feature release.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for the void and start building for the market. By instrumenting your product now, you eliminate the guesswork that kills most startups. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
